name: codex
description: Get a second opinion from OpenAI's Codex CLI when stuck on difficult debugging, code analysis, or architecture problems, run a code/diff review (finder and adversarial-refuter passes), run a security review/audit or verification of security-sensitive code (auth, input validation, crypto, secrets), diagnose a bug, hand it a well-scoped edit or clearly-bounded implementation task, generate images with Codex's $imagegen skill, offload rote throwaway work where nothing can go wrong, or run a recon sweep (enumerations, config/wiring locates, traces, pattern sweeps, routine chores). Runs gpt-5.6-sol at xhigh reasoning on the fast service tier (gpt-5.6-luna for rote/bulk and the recon lane, which now defaults to luna; the fast tier is always pinned). The skill runs inline (no context fork), so it is safe to invoke from the main conversation, subagents, and workflows; also ships a codex-wrapper agent for workflow stages that route to codex by agent type. Every invocation runs through the bundled codex-ask executable (on the Bash tool's PATH via the plugin's bin/), which pins the flags, keeps temp files on absolute scratch paths, survives a Bash-tool timeout (recover with `codex-ask --await`), mints a scratchpad-adopting fan-out root and lane roster (`codex-ask --mint-root`), and reconciles a fan-out from disk (`codex-ask --collect` classifies each lane's on-disk state — the deliverable is the disk record, not the agent's narration). Requires the `codex` CLI on PATH. Ships a captain-hook dispatch guard that keeps every dispatch foreground and routed through codex-ask — it blocks a backgrounded or detached codex-ask (run_in_background, a trailing `&`, or nohup/setsid/disown) and a hand-rolled `codex exec`. Async dispatch is first-class instead: `--dispatch` returns at once; an owner subagent adds `--owner <agent-id>` and parks on the plugin's `await` steering tool (a per-user daemon wakes it on completion, with a parent-relay fallback), a top-level session arms Monitor on `codex-ask --watch`, and the codex-wrapper-async agent packages the owner flow for fan-outs.
